title: Wiborgia humilis
intro: Wiborgia humilis, a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, is native to the Western Cape province of South Africa. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and wounds. Bioactive constituents of Wiborgia humilis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Wiborgia humilis is often prepared as a tea or infused in alcohol to treat respiratory conditions, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Wiborgia humilis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It belongs to the genus Wiborgia, which is endemic to southern Africa. The genus Wiborgia is part of the order Lamiales. The plant's classification is a part of its scientific name, which is a combination of its genus and species names. This classification system is used in botany to identify and organize plant species.

The plant's roots are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and digestive issues.
